cli.help: Remux mode imports audio tracks and subtitles from MKV files in different languages.
Split mode cuts MKV files into segments or episodes.
--lang en|it                  CLI/UI language for this run
-ext, --extensions <list>      File extensions to scan. Default: mkv
-t,   --target-language <code> ISO 639-2 language code(s), e.g. ita or eng,ita
-l,   --language <path>        Folder with MKV files to import tracks from
If omitted, the source folder is used
-fs,  --framesync              Visual frame comparison for constant delay
-da,  --deep-analysis          Full analysis for files with different edits
Common: ita, eng, jpn, ger/deu, fra/fre, spa, por, rus, chi/zho, kor

web.config.validation.audioSourceThresho: Audio source: threshold is required and must be greater than 0
web.config.validation.audioSourceLanguag: Audio source: source language is required
web.config.validation.audioSourceModeReq: Audio source: select at least one mode
web.config.validation.audioSourceFormatR: Audio source: audio format is required
web.config.validation.audioSourceScopeRe: Audio source: audio must be Lang or All
web.config.validation.sourceRawMustBeFil: Source raw: must be a file, not a folder
web.config.validation.splitAtRequired: Split at: required for split-at mode
web.config.validation.trimRequired: Trim: set trim start, trim end, or both
web.config.help.split.source.title: Source
web.config.help.split.source.text: Required. Select a single MKV file or a folder. With a folder, the scan prepares a batch using the same options for all MKV files found.
web.config.help.split.output.title: Output
web.config.help.split.output.text: Optional. Must be a folder. If it remains empty, each output is written to the input file folder.
web.config.help.split.sourceRaw.title: Source raw
web.config.help.split.sourceRaw.text: Optional and single-file only. Use it when the input is a re-encode and you want to read PTS from the original VFR source. Must be a file, not a folder.
web.config.help.split.recursive.title: Recursive
web.config.help.split.recursive.text: Used only when Source is a folder. Includes MKV files in subfolders during the scan.
web.config.help.split.force.title: Force
web.config.help.split.force.text: If enabled, overwrites existing outputs. If disabled, segments that already exist are skipped.
web.config.help.split.dryRun.title: Dry-run
web.config.help.split.dryRun.text: Shows segments and pipeline without writing files.
web.config.help.split.mode.title: Mode
web.config.help.split.mode.text: Required. Cut modes are alternatives: choose only one. Form fields change based on the selected mode.
web.config.help.split.pattern.title: Pattern
web.config.help.split.pattern.text: Required in Chapter pattern mode. Example: 5,5,5,6. The sum must match the number of chapters in the file. Naming is managed only with Template.
web.config.help.split.ranges.title: Ranges
web.config.help.split.ranges.text: Required in Ranges mode. Explicit intervals separated by comma. Example: 00:00:00-00:21:40,00:21:40-END.
web.config.help.split.splitAt.title: Split at
web.config.help.split.splitAt.text: Required in Split at mode. List of cut points; generates N+1 segments. Example: 20:00,40:00.
web.config.help.split.trimStart.title: Trim start
web.config.help.split.trimStart.text: In Trim mode, drops everything before this point. Can be filled together with Trim end.
web.config.help.split.trimEnd.title: Trim end
web.config.help.split.trimEnd.text: In Trim mode, drops everything after this point. Can be filled together with Trim start.
web.config.help.split.snap.title: Snap
web.config.help.split.snap.text: off keeps frame-perfect cutting. before/after/nearest move the start to a keyframe and avoid re-encoding the initial GOP.
web.config.help.split.extensions.title: Extensions
web.config.help.split.template.title: Template
web.config.help.split.template.text: Optional. If empty, automatic naming is used.
web.config.help.remux.source.title: Source
web.config.help.remux.source.text: Folder with source MKV files. Remux works by episode scan/matching.
web.config.help.remux.language.title: Language
web.config.help.remux.language.text: Folder with MKV files to import audio/subtitle tracks from. If empty, uses Source also as the language folder.
web.config.help.remux.destination.title: Destination
web.config.help.remux.destination.text: Output folder. As an alternative, Overwrite source can be used.
web.config.help.remux.overwrite.title: Overwrite source
web.config.help.remux.overwrite.text: Writes the result in place of the source file. Alternative to the Destination folder.
web.config.help.remux.recursive.title: Recursive
web.config.help.remux.target.title: Target language
web.config.help.remux.target.text: Languages of audio/subtitle tracks to import from the Language file.
Accepts three-letter ISO 639-2 codes recognized by MKV metadata.
web.config.help.remux.audioCodec.title: Audio codec
web.config.help.remux.audioCodec.text: Optional filter on audio codecs to import from the Language file. Empty = imports all audio codecs of the target language.
Format: codecs or aliases separated by comma.
DTS matches only the DTS core, not DTS-HD MA.
DTS-HD matches DTS-HD MA and DTS-HD HR.
web.config.help.remux.keepAudio.title: Keep audio
web.config.help.remux.keepAudio.text: Audio languages to keep from the original Source. Empty = keeps all source audio tracks that are not replaced by the imported language.
Accepts three-letter ISO 639-2 codes recognized by MKV metadata.
web.config.help.remux.keepCodec.title: Keep codec
web.config.help.remux.keepCodec.text: Optional filter on audio codecs to keep from the original Source. Empty = no codec filter on kept source audio tracks.
Format: codecs or aliases separated by comma.
DTS matches only the DTS core, not DTS-HD MA.
DTS-HD matches DTS-HD MA and DTS-HD HR.
web.config.help.remux.keepSub.title: Keep sub
web.config.help.remux.keepSub.text: Subtitle languages to keep from the original Source. Empty = keeps all source subtitles.
Accepts three-letter ISO 639-2 codes recognized by MKV metadata.
web.config.help.remux.subOnly.title: Subtitles only
web.config.help.remux.subOnly.text: Imports only subtitles from the language file, ignoring audio.
web.config.help.remux.audioOnly.title: Audio only
web.config.help.remux.audioOnly.text: Imports only audio from the language file, ignoring subtitles.
web.config.help.remux.speed.title: Speed
web.config.help.remux.speed.text: Speed correction through temporal stretch, without audio/video re-encoding.
Auto detects the factor only when CFR metadata is reliable.
Manual applies the value specified in Stretch.
PAL/NTSC is only a typical case, for example 25025/24000 or 24000/25025. In manual mode you can use any valid factor.
web.config.help.remux.stretch.title: Stretch
web.config.help.remux.stretch.text: Accepts a decimal number or a fraction.
web.config.help.remux.framesync.title: Frame-sync
web.config.help.remux.framesync.text: Calculates a fixed offset by comparing scene cuts. Does not fix edits/cuts in the middle.
web.config.help.remux.deep.title: Deep analysis
web.config.help.remux.deep.text: Full analysis for releases with added/removed scenes. Mutually exclusive with Frame-sync.

If it finds only a constant delay, it applies --sync without audio post-processing. If it produces cut/insert operations and you import audio, an Audio format is required to re-encode the affected tracks.
web.config.help.remux.cropSource.title: Crop src
web.config.help.remux.cropSource.text: Manual crop applied only to Source frames used for visual matching.
Format: L:R:T:B in pixels on the original frame before scaling. Empty or 0:0:0:0 disables it. Does not crop the final output.
web.config.help.remux.cropLang.title: Crop lang
web.config.help.remux.cropLang.text: Manual crop applied only to Language frames used for visual matching.
Format: L:R:T:B in pixels on the original frame before scaling. Empty or 0:0:0:0 disables it. Does not crop the final output.
web.config.help.remux.audioDelay.title: Audio delay
web.config.help.remux.audioDelay.text: Manual offset in milliseconds added to the automatic synchronization result.
web.config.help.remux.subDelay.title: Sub delay
web.config.help.remux.subDelay.text: Manual offset in milliseconds for imported subtitles.
web.config.help.remux.audioSourceFill.te: Enables filling missing parts using an audio track already present in the Source.
Appears only after choosing an Audio format. When enabled, it requires Threshold, Source language, at least one mode, and Audio set to Lang or All.
web.config.help.remux.audioSourceFillThr: Required with Audio source fill enabled. When an enabled condition exceeds this threshold, RemuxForge takes that specific audio segment from the Source and concatenates it to the track imported from the Language file.
web.config.help.remux.audioSourceFillLan: Language of the Source audio track to take fill segments from. Required with Audio source fill enabled.
Accepts three-letter ISO 639-2 codes recognized by MKV metadata.
web.config.help.remux.audioSourceFillSta: Applies filling at the beginning of the file.
If the positive initial audio delay exceeds the Audio source fill threshold, RemuxForge takes the first N milliseconds from the Source and prepends them to the track imported from the Language file.
Imported subtitles are not concatenated: they keep the calculated delay.
web.config.help.remux.audioSourceFillEnd: Applies filling at the end of the audio track.
The condition is: source audio track duration - (Language track duration + applied delay) > threshold.
If there is no reference audio track in the Source, it uses the video track duration.
web.config.help.remux.audioSourceFillIns: If Deep analysis detects an INSERT_SILENCE operation greater than the Audio source fill threshold, RemuxForge does not insert silence: it takes the corresponding audio segment from the Source and concatenates it to the imported track.
web.config.help.remux.match.title: Pattern match
web.config.help.remux.match.text: Regex used to match episodes between Source and Language. Captured groups form the episode ID: two files match when they produce the same ID.
web.config.help.remux.extensions.title: Extensions
web.config.help.remux.extensions.text: Extensions searched during scan. Default: mkv. Multiple values separated by comma.
web.config.help.remux.audioFormat.title: Audio format
web.config.help.remux.audioFormat.text: Final format for processed audio tracks: FLAC, LPCM, AAC or Opus.
Enables dependent audio options. Required for Audio source fill and for Deep analysis only when the analysis produces operations that modify audio.
web.config.help.remux.audioScope.title: Audio
web.config.help.remux.audioScope.text: Chooses which audio tracks to encode in the selected format: Lang or All.
Required with Audio source fill enabled. Disabled avoids generic conversions; any required Deep analysis operations still require the audio format.
web.config.help.remux.audio24.title: 24bit -> 16bit
web.config.help.remux.audio24.text: Reduces 24-bit tracks to 16-bit using soxr VHQ and shibata dither. Available only for FLAC and LPCM.
web.config.help.remux.audioNormalize.tex: Global multichannel peak normalization. The target must be less than or equal to 0 dB.
web.config.help.remux.rename.title: Audio rename
web.config.help.remux.rename.text: Renames final audio tracks using codec, channel layout and effective parameters. AAC and Opus do not include bit depth.
web.config.help.remux.encoding.title: Video encoding
web.config.help.remux.encoding.text: Optional post-merge video re-encoding profile. Profiles are managed from Settings > Encoding profiles.
web.merge.ready: Ready. Press F2 to configure, F5 to scan.
web.merge.busyRetry: Operation in progress: try again after processing finishes
web.merge.configNotApplicable: Configuration cannot be applied: check the log
web.merge.configAppliedScanInvalidated: Configuration applied: previous scan invalidated, press F5
web.merge.scanCompleted: Scan completed: {0} files found, {1} ready, {2} skipped
web.merge.stopRequested: Stop requested: the operation will stop at the next safe point
web.split.ready: Split ready. Select a source file or folder.
web.split.errorSummary: Split finished with errors: {0} completed, {1} errors
